name: Dr Haley Sapers
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
bio: Haley Sapers is an Assistant Professor of Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University whose research lies at the intersection of planetary science, geobiology, and astrobiology. Her work focuses on understanding how geological and biological processes interact, particularly in extreme environments, with implications for the search for life beyond Earth. Dr. Sapers studies meteorite impact craters as key environments that can create and preserve habitable conditions, as well as methane dynamics and microbial ecosystems in analog sites on Earth that resemble Martian environments. She integrates fieldwork, laboratory analysis, and remote sensing to investigate planetary habitability and biosignatures. Prior to joining NAU, she earned her Ph.D. in planetary science from Western University and completed postdoctoral research with NASA’s Astrobiology “Deep Life” program, contributing to studies of subsurface microbial life and Mars exploration.
